title: 10 best herbal capsules for alzheimer’s disease
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for alzheimer’s disease are natural supplements that help alleviate symptoms and slow down the progression of this neurodegenerative disorder. They work by improving cognitive function, memory, and reducing inflammation in the brain. The best herbal capsules for alzheimer’s disease include Bacopa monnieri, Ginkgo biloba, Curcuma longa, Centella asiatica, Panax ginseng, Salvia miltiorrhiza, Zingiber officinale, Withania somnifera, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Ocimum sanctum. If left untreated, alzheimer’s disease can lead to memory loss, confusion, and difficulty performing daily tasks, ultimately affecting one’s independence, relationships, and overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory. The capsules' active ingredients, such as californianum extract, have been found to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are key contributing factors to the progression of Alzheimer's. Additionally, maidenhair tree extracts have been demonstrated to increase blood flow to the brain, promoting healthy neuronal activity and potentially slowing down cognitive decline associated with the disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba extracts have potent antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the brain, which is thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Vasodilatory effects: Maidenhair Tree capsules can improve blood flow to the brain by dilating blood vessels, which may help restore cognitive function and memory loss associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective compounds: Ginkgo biloba extracts contain a range of neuroprotective compounds, including flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, that have been shown to inhibit the production of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the development and progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Bilobalide: This terpene has been shown to improve cognitive function and memory by increasing the production of neurotransmitters such as acetylcholine, which is involved in the transmission of nerve impulses.

Ginkgolides: These diterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the damage caused by beta-amyloid plaques and neurofibrillary tangles, key features of Alzheimer's disease pathology.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve memory and cognitive function.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds, also known as ginkgo nuts, contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain.

Buds: Ginkgo buds, particularly the young leaves and shoots, are also used in traditional medicine to improve memory and cognitive function, possibly due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer when they are fully ripe and have a yellowish-green color.
Dry the leaves in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind 2 ounces of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of water to form a gel.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the gel mixture and seal the capsules to create ginkgo biloba capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a natural extract of Bacopa monnieri, a plant that has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to enhance cognitive function and memory. The capsules are believed to promote the growth of new neurons and improve communication between brain cells, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as memory loss, confusion, and difficulty with problem-solving and language skills.
remedy_properties: Nootropic activity: Brahmi capsules have been shown to possess nootropic properties, which improve memory and cognitive function by enhancing neurotransmitter production and transmission, thereby helping to slow down cognitive decline in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Brahmi capsules help protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease and related cognitive decline.

Neuroprotective activity: Brahmi capsules have been found to exhibit neuroprotective effects, which help safeguard against neuronal damage and death, potentially slowing down or halting the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of glycosides that have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function by enhancing the synthesis of neurotransmitters and neuronal communication, which is essential for learning and memory.

Bacopasides: Bacopasides are another group of compounds found in Bacopa monnieri that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation that are associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Monnierin: Monnierin is a phenylethylamine alkaloid that has been found to have neuroprotective properties, including the ability to inhibit the formation of amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and to improve memory and cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of active compounds, particularly bacosides, which are believed to improve memory and cognitive function.
Roots: Utilized for their ability to enhance memory, learning, and neural protection, as they contain bacosides and other bioactive molecules.
Stems: Employed for their rich content of bacosides, which may help improve memory, cognitive function, and neural plasticity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried bacopa monnieri roots and leaves according to the recommended quantity.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder to achieve uniform particle size.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of glycerin to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty capsules with the powder blend using an automatic capsule filler and seal immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain curcumin, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain. These anti-inflammatory properties may help slow down or even halt the progression of Alzheimer's by preventing the accumulation of amyloid plaques, which are characteristic of the disease. Additionally, curcumin may also improve cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and reducing symptoms such as memory loss and confusion.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Bioavailability-enhancing compounds: Turmeric capsules often contain bioavailability-enhancing compounds like piperine (black pepper extract) that increase the absorption of curcuminoids into the bloodstream, allowing them to reach the brain more effectively and have a greater therapeutic impact on cognitive function.

Neuroprotective flavonoids: Turmeric contains a range of neuroprotective flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which can help protect against beta-amyloid plaque formation and tau protein aggregation in the brain, both hallmark features of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with Alzheimer's disease by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, thereby preventing neurodegeneration and improving cognitive function.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help prevent the progression of Alzheimer's disease by inhibiting the formation of amyloid plaques and reducing neuronal damage.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It has neuroprotective properties that help prevent the death of neurons and reduce inflammation in the brain, thereby slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease and improving cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also used, as they contain bioactive compounds that may help improve cognitive function and reduce oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Seeds: While less commonly reported than rhyzomes and roots, seeds of Curcuma longa are sometimes used due to their potential neuroprotective and antioxidant effects, which may help slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest curcuma longa rhizomes after 8-10 months of cultivation when they are fully matured.
Clean the harvested rhizomes thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned rhizomes in a well-ventilated area at a temperature of 40-50 degrees celsius for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried curcuma longa rhizomes into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the prepared powder and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory. These compounds, including ginsenosides and polysaccharides, may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, ginseng has been found to enhance blood flow to the brain, promoting healthy neuronal activity and potentially slowing down the degeneration of brain cells associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Ginseng's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to Alzheimer's disease progression.

Neuroprotective property: Ginseng has been shown to exhibit neuroprotective effects by reducing beta-amyloid plaque formation, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and protecting against neuronal damage.

Cognitive-enhancing property: Ginseng has been found to improve cognitive function and memory in individuals with Alzheimer's disease, possibly due to its ability to increase blood flow to the brain and enhance neurotransmitter activity.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpene saponins have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Ginsenoside Rg1: This ginsenoside has been shown to improve cognitive function and memory by increasing the expression of neurotrophic factors and promoting neuronal survival, which can help slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Ginsenoside Rb1: This ginsenoside has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease and improving cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in ginsenosides, which are believed to have neuroprotective effects that can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes also contain ginsenosides, which may help slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
Stems: Although less commonly mentioned, stems of Panax ginseng are also used to extract ginsenosides, which can contribute to the neuroprotective effects of the capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of panax ginseng roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the roots for 2-3 hours at 50-60 degrees celsius in a dehydrator.
Grind 5 grams of dried ginseng roots into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix the powder with 2-3 grams of a natural binder like gelatin and fill a capsule mold.
Fill 50-100 capsules with the ginseng mixture and seal them tightly in a clean environment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a powerful antioxidant called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function. This natural compound has also been found to reduce inflammation in the brain, a common characteristic of the disease. Additionally, sage capsules have been demonstrated to increase acetylcholine levels, a neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in memory formation and retrieval, thereby helping to slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Rosmarinic acid: This compound has been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to neurodegeneration associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Terpenes: Terpenes have been found to improve memory and cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and protecting against neuronal damage.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, may help prevent or slow the progression of Alzheimer's disease by reducing amyloid plaque formation and improving insulin signaling in the brain.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has neuroprotective properties, which may help reduce neuroinflammation, improve cognitive function, and prevent neuronal damage associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Salvianolic acid A: This phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease, and improve cognitive function and memory.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of antioxidants and neuroprotective compounds like carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Seeds: They contain a rich source of fatty acids and flavonoids, which may contribute to their potential neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to mitigate Alzheimer's symptoms.

Roots: They are used for their saponin content, which may have neuroprotective properties and help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, potentially slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested material at 50c for 2 hours in a temperature-controlled drying oven.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of the powdered salvia officinalis with 25mg of a binding agent in a bowl.
Fill 50 empty capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain the essential oil of the herb, which has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function. The active compounds in rosemary, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been found to increase blood flow to the brain, boost neurotransmitter production, and even prevent the formation of beta-amyloid plaques - a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease. This natural approach can provide an effective way to support brain health and potentially slow down or even halt the progression of this devastating condition.
remedy_properties: Carnosic acid: Carnosic acid, a polyphenolic compound found in rosemary, has been shown to prevent beta-amyloid plaques from forming and reducing existing ones, which are characteristic of Alzheimer's disease.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, another major component of rosemary, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain that can contribute to Alzheimer's development.

Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid, a triterpene compound found in rosemary, has been shown to have neuroprotective effects by reducing apoptosis (cell death) and inhibiting inflammatory pathways in the brain, which may help prevent or slow down neuronal damage associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: This monoterpenic phenol has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, potentially by inhibiting amyloid-beta aggregation, a key factor in Alzheimer's disease pathology.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic diterpene has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as the ability to cross the blood-brain barrier, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of antioxidants and carnosic acid, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Stems: Used for their rosmarinic acid content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Flowers: Used due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may have neuroprotective effects and help improve cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Dry the rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a single layer for 2 hours at 105f to preserve their properties.
Grind 100g of dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 100g of the powdered leaves with 50g of gelatin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 empty capsules with the gelatin mixture for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goat's rue capsules helps with Alzheimer’s disease because it has been traditionally used to improve cognitive function, memory, and mood. The herb contains a unique compound called saponin, which has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, helping to prevent the degeneration of brain cells and reduce inflammation. Additionally, goat's rue is believed to increase blood flow to the brain, allowing for more oxygen and nutrients to reach damaged areas, potentially slowing down the progression of Alzheimer’s disease.
remedy_properties: Choline: Goat's Rue capsules contain Choline, which is converted into Acetylcholine in the brain, a neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in memory formation and can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

Flavonoids and Phenolic Compounds: These compounds have antioxidant properties that can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Triterpenoid Saponins: Goat's Rue capsules contain Triterpenoid Saponins, which may help reduce beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark feature of Alzheimer's disease, by inhibiting the production of beta-amyloid peptides and reducing inflammation in the brain.
remedy_constituents: Aldose Reductase Inhibitors (ARI): ARI found in Galega officinalis may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease by inhibiting the activity of aldose reductase enzyme.

Phenolic Compounds (e.g., Gallic Acid, Quercetin): Phenolic compounds in Galega officinalis may help protect against neurodegeneration and inflammation in Alzheimer's disease by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

Iridoid Glycosides (e.g., secologanin): Iridoid glycosides in Galega officinalis may have neuroprotective effects by scavenging free radicals, modulating neurotransmitter levels, and reducing neuroinflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Galega officinalis are commonly used because they contain a high concentration of medicinal compounds, particularly galegine, which has been studied for its potential neuroprotective effects in Alzheimer's disease.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may contribute to their neuroprotective properties and potential benefits for Alzheimer's disease.

Stems: The stems of Galega officinalis are also used, possibly due to their similar composition to the roots and leaves, which may provide a consistent source of medicinal compounds for Alzheimer's treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the galega officinalis roots and leaves at the peak of potency and dry them for 7 days to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind the dried galega officinalis roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 400 milligrams of the ground galega officinalis powder with 200 milligrams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler or spoon.
Store the herbal galega officinalis capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory. The herb's potent antioxidants and flavonoids help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are common contributors to neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort has been found to enhance blood flow to the brain, promoting healthy neuronal communication and potentially reversing damage caused by the disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Centella asiatica's antioxidant properties help to protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are thought to contribute to the development and progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective compounds: The herb contains a range of neuroprotective compounds, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, which may help to prevent neuronal damage and death caused by Alzheimer's-related factors such as beta-amyloid plaques and tau protein aggregation.

Immunomodulatory effects: Centella asiatica has been shown to modulate the immune response in a way that may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which could slow down or even halt the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids (asiaticoside and madecassoside): These compounds help by reducing beta-amyloid plaques and improving memory and cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
Madecassoside: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is associated with the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Asiatic Acid: This compound has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, reducing oxidative stress and apoptosis (cell death) in neurons, which helps slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Roots: Utilized for their possible neuroprotective effects, which may help prevent or slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
Stems: Employed for their potential in improving memory and cognitive function, possibly due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried centella asiatica leaves and flowers from a reputable supplier.
Dry the centella asiatica at 40°c for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 100g of the dried centella asiatica into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix 10g of the centella asiatica powder with 10g of gelatin and 10g of glycerin.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Club moss capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain Huperzine A, a natural compound that has been shown to inhibit the breakdown of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter essential for memory and cognitive function. This helps to improve cognitive impairment and slow down the progression of the disease. Additionally, club moss capsules have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, further contributing to its neuroprotective effects against Alzheimer's.
remedy_properties: Huperzine A: Inhibits acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter essential for memory and cognitive function, thereby increasing its levels in the brain.
Flavonoids: Exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, common underlying factors of Alzheimer's disease.
Lignans: Have been shown to possess cholinergic activity, which can help to improve memory and cognitive function by modulating the neurotransmitter acetylcholine.
remedy_constituents: Huperzine A: Huperzine A is a potent acetylcholinesterase inhibitor that helps increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, thereby improving cognitive function and memory in patients with Alzheimer's disease.

Lycopodium acid: Lycopodium acid has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, contributing to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Lycopodium alkaloids: Lycopodium alkaloids, specifically including lycopodine and huperizinine, have been found to have neuroprotective effects, which help prevent neuronal damage and death, and promote the survival of neurons in the brain, thereby slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Huperzia serrata leaves are used due to their high content of huperzine A, a natural cholinesterase inhibitor that helps improve memory and cognitive function.
Roots: Huperzia serrata roots are used for their rich content of huperzine A, which can help prevent the breakdown of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in memory and learning.
Stems: Huperzia serrata stems are used due to their huperzine A content, which can help increase the concentration of neurotransmitters in the brain, thereby improving cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried huperzia serrata leaves and stems from a reliable source.
Grind the huperzia serrata into a fine powder using a grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the huperzia serrata powder with 1 gram of a carrier such as starch or cellulose.
Fill empty capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a potent combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that have been shown to have neuroprotective properties. These compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of the disease. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender may also help to reduce anxiety and agitation, common symptoms associated with Alzheimer's. By addressing these underlying issues, English lavender capsules may help to slow down cognitive decline and improve overall quality of life for individuals with Alzheimer's.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lavender's antioxidants can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which is a key contributor to Alzheimer's disease progression.

Cholinergic activity: Lavender's cholinergic effects may help increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, which is an essential neurotransmitter that plays a role in memory formation and recall, potentially improving cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's.

Anxiolytic and calming effects: Lavender's calming properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, both of which are common symptoms of Alzheimer's disease, and may also contribute to improved sleep quality, which is often disrupted in individuals with the condition.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with Alzheimer's disease by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of the disease.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which can help prevent or slow the progression of Alzheimer's disease by protecting brain cells from damage.

Apigenin: This phenolic compound has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the risk of developing Alzheimer's disease by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in bioactive compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have potential therapeutic effects in treating Alzheimer's disease.
Leaves: The leaves contain essential oils and flavonoids that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's disease, promoting neuroprotective effects.

Seeds: Lavandula angustifolia seeds are a rich source of antioxidants and bioactive compounds, which may help protect against neurodegenerative diseases, including Alzheimer's disease, by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at 9am when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers at 40°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the powder and seal immediately to prevent moisture exposure.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a herb that has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The capsules may also help to calm inflammation in the brain, which is often associated with the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Maypop has been shown to improve sleep quality, which is essential for memory consolidation and learning, making it a potential natural supplement to support cognitive function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maypop capsules have antioxidant effects that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to Alzheimer's disease progression.

GABA-enhancing properties: Maypop contains compounds that enhance GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid) activity, a neurotransmitter involved in regulating sleep, anxiety, and relaxation; this may help improve cognitive function and reduce symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

Acetylcholine-boosting properties: Maypop has been shown to increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, a neurotransmitter involved in memory formation and attention; boosting acetylcholine levels can potentially slow down cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Rhyzomes: Rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess neuroprotective effects and improve cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried passiflora incarnata flowers from a reputable supplier or grow your own plants in a sunny spot for 6 hours daily.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using an electric grinder or mortar and pestle within 24 hours of harvesting to preserve potency.
Mix 250 milliliters of vodka with 1 gram of the ground passiflora incarnata powder in a clean glass jar, sealing it tightly for 2 weeks to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid extract through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids and setting aside for capsule filling within 30 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 200 milligrams of the extracted liquid per serving, storing them in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules help with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a natural compound called valerenic acid, which has been shown to possess neuroprotective properties. This property may help protect brain cells from damage caused by the disease, slowing down its progression and improving cognitive function. Additionally, valerian capsules have been found to increase blood flow to the brain, allowing for better oxygenation and nutrient delivery, which can help improve memory and cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant properties help protect the brain from oxidative stress, which can contribute to Alzheimer's disease by reducing the formation of beta-amyloid plaques.

Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Valerian's ability to inhibit acetylcholinesterase (an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine) may help increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, which can improve memory and cognitive function impaired by Alzheimer's disease.

GABA receptor activation: Valerian's activiation of GABA receptors can promote a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help reduce anxiety and agitation symptoms common in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valepotriates, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective against cognitive decline.
Rhyzomes: Containing isovaleric acid, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly removing any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm place or using a food dehydrator on low setting for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of ground valeriana officinalis with 100mg of silica gel in a small bowl for 2 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potential to enhance cognitive function and memory. The herb contains compounds that have been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with the disease. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects may help alleviate anxiety and agitation, common symptoms of Alzheimer's. As a result, studies suggest that supplementing with herbal lemon balm capsules may slow cognitive decline and improve overall quality of life for individuals suffering from this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Lemon balm capsules contain antioxidant compounds that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage brain cells and contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

GABA-like effects: Lemon balm contains rosmarinic acid, a compound that has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which can help calm anxiety, improve sleep quality, and reduce symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Lemon balm capsules may help increase the production of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that is often depleted in individuals with Alzheimer's disease, by inhibiting the enzyme acetylcholinesterase, which breaks down acetylcholine. This can improve cognitive function and memory.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant have been traditionally used to treat cognitive impairment and memory loss due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are rich in essential oils and flavonoids that have neuroprotective effects, making them an effective ingredient for alleviating symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and dry them using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered melissa officinalis with 10 grams of pure bee pollen in a bowl until well combined.
Fill empty capsules with 500mg of the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have neuroprotective properties. Research suggests that hyperforin may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, hyperforin may also increase levels of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which can help regulate mood and cognitive function. This natural extract may provide a safe and effective adjunct therapy for managing mild to moderate Alzheimer's symptoms.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have neuroprotective effects by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Hypaconitine: This alkaloid has been found to inhibit the breakdown of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that is often impaired in individuals with Alzheimer's disease, helping to improve cognitive function and memory.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort capsules have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against damage caused by free radicals in the brain and may slow down the progression of neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which can help mitigate the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Buds: The buds contain high levels of hypericins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful in combating oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves at dawn or before noon on a sunny day.
Dry the leaves in a single layer on paper bags or trays for 7-10 days.
Grind dried hypericum perforatum leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of powdered hypericum perforatum with 1000 milliliters of distilled water to create a tincture solution that will take 2 weeks to steep at room temperature.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the tincture solution after 14 days and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a powerful herb that has been shown to have neuroprotective properties. The herb, Scutellaria lateriflora, has been found to increase the production of a protein called BDNF, which is essential for the growth and survival of brain cells. By promoting the health and function of these cells, skullcap capsules may help to slow down or even halt the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Baicalin: Baicalin, a flavonoid compound found in skullcap, has been shown to inhibit amyloid-β protein aggregation, which is a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease. This may help prevent the formation of toxic protein clumps that can damage brain cells.

Scutellarin: Scutellarin, another flavonoid in skullcap, has been found to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Borneol: Borneol, a terpene compound in skullcap, has been shown to exhibit neuroprotective effects by blocking the production of inflammatory cytokines and inhibiting the activation of pro-apoptotic pathways, which can help prevent neuronal damage and death.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in combating oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes are rich in iridoid glycosides, particularly baicalin, which has been found to inhibit the production of beta-amyloid peptides, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality scutellaria baicalensis roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a hot air circulation oven at 60°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 500 grams of dried scutellaria baicalensis root into a fine powder using a mill or food processor.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered root with 125 grams of potato starch and 25 grams of magnesium stearate, then compress into capsules.
Package 500 capsules in amber glass bottles, store them in a cool dark place, and use within 2 years from date of manufacture.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to Alzheimer's disease that are treated with herbal capsules are Vascular Dementia, Lewy Body Disease, Frontotemporal Dementia, and Mixed Dementia. Herbal supplements such as Bacopa monnieri, Ginkgo biloba, and Phosphatidylserine have been traditionally used to improve memory, cognitive function, and neuroprotection in these conditions. These herbs may help reduce symptoms of dementia, including forgetfulness, confusion, and difficulty with communication and daily activities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a powerful herb that enhances cognitive function and memory. This herb is known to improve the brain's ability to form new memories and retrieve existing ones. Bacopa monnieri capsules have been shown to increase the production of neurotransmitters that facilitate communication between brain cells, leading to improved memory, learning, and concentration. Additionally, they have antioxidant properties that help protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage associated with Alzheimer's disease. This makes them a promising natural remedy for managing the symptoms of the condition.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Help improve memory and cognitive function in Alzheimer's disease by enhancing the activity of neurotransmitters and promoting the growth of new neurons.
Bacosterol: Have antioxidant properties that protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are associated with the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
Bacoside-A: Act as a neuroprotective agent by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that break down neurotransmitters, thereby maintaining healthy neural communication and function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bioactive compounds like bacosides, which have neuroprotective properties that help in improving cognitive function.
Roots: They contain a high concentration of bacosides, which are responsible for the medicinal properties of Bacopa monnieri, including its ability to improve memory and cognitive function.

Stems: They also contain bacosides and other active compounds that contribute to the plant's potential benefits in Alzheimer's disease treatment, such as improving memory and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried bacopa monnieri roots and leaves from a trusted supplier.
Grind 200 grams of the harvested material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground powder with 50 grams of gum arabic and 25 grams of silicon dioxide.
Use a capsule filler to fill 500 empty capsules with 250 milligrams of the powder mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help improve blood flow to the brain, enhancing cognitive function and memory. The capsules contain extracts from the Ginkgo biloba leaves, which have been shown to inhibit the formation of amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has been found to have neuroprotective properties, helping to prevent the damage of brain cells and promoting overall brain health, making it a potentially valuable supplement in the management of Alzheimer's.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help by improving blood flow to the brain, thereby enhancing cognitive function and reducing symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Terpenoids (Ginkgolides): They help by inhibiting the formation of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and reducing inflammation in the brain.
Bilobalide: They help by protecting neurons from damage, reducing oxidative stress, and improving communication between neurons, which can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are the most commonly used part due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve memory and cognitive function.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used in capsules for Alzheimer's disease because they contain a high concentration of ginkgotoxine, a compound that may help to improve cognitive function.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba trees are also used, as they contain a higher concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids compared to the leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of ginkgo biloba leaves from a mature tree and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Crush the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle with 2 tablespoons of silica.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250mg of the ginkgo biloba powder and seal them securely with a capsule machine.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin has been shown to reduce the progression of neurodegenerative diseases, including Alzheimer's, by inhibiting the formation of amyloid plaques and tau tangles in the brain. It also enhances brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF), promoting neuronal health and survival, thereby slowing down cognitive decline and improving memory and cognitive function in individuals suffering from Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help with Alzheimer's disease by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are key factors in the progression of the disease.
Voltilin: It helps with Alzheimer's disease by inhibiting the production of amyloid beta plaques, a hallmark of the disease, and reducing neuroinflammation.

Volatile oils (specifically Turmerone): They help with Alzheimer's disease by promoting the growth of new neurons and reducing inflammation, which can improve cognitive function and slow down disease progression.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain curcumin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate Alzheimer's symptoms.
Roots: The roots are also a source of curcumin and other beneficial compounds, which contribute to its potential therapeutic effects in Alzheimer's disease.
Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa contain various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, that may have neuroprotective effects and help manage Alzheimer's symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried turmeric root at the end of the harvest season when the root is fully mature.
Grind the dried turmeric root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered turmeric root with 5 grams of a natural binding agent like gum arabic.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the turmeric root mixture using a capsule filler for 3 minutes.
Label and store the filled herbal curcuma longa capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain compounds that improve memory and cognitive function. The herb has been traditionally used to enhance blood flow to the brain, promoting healthy neuronal communication and function. Studies have shown that Centella asiatica extracts can increase the production of neurotrophic factors, which support the growth and survival of neurons, thereby slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease and improving overall brain health. This natural supplement offers a promising alternative for managing this complex condition.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help with Alzheimer's disease by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may protect against neurodegeneration and improve cognitive function.
Phenolic acids: Assist in Alzheimer's disease management by scavenging free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and potentially inhibiting the formation of amyloid plaques.
Asiatic acid: May help alleviate Alzheimer's disease symptoms by modulating neurotransmitter release, reducing inflammation, and promoting neuroprotection in the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Centella asiatica are most commonly used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as asiatic acid and madecassic acid, which have been shown to have neuroprotective effects.
Roots: The roots are also widely used for their triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate Alzheimer's symptoms.

Stems: The stems of Centella asiatica contain a significant amount of saponins, which have been found to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh centella asiatica leaves and flowers to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested centella asiatica in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried centella asiatica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix the centella asiatica powder with 500g of gelatin to create a capsule filling mixture.
Fill the capsule shells with the centella asiatica and gelatin mixture using a manual capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potential to improve cognitive function and memory. The bioactive compounds in ginseng, such as ginsenosides, may help to enhance blood flow to the brain, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. This can lead to improved mental clarity, focus, and reduced symptoms of dementia. Additionally, ginseng has been found to have neuroprotective properties, which can help to preserve brain cells and promote overall brain health.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Help improve cognitive function and memory by reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and beta-amyloid plaque formation in the brain, which are associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Saponins: Exhibit neuroprotective effects by reducing apoptosis (cell death), improving neuronal survival, and enhancing synaptic plasticity, which are crucial for learning and memory.
Panaxynol: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, thereby slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng are widely used due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which have been shown to have neuroprotective effects.
Barks: The barks of Panax ginseng are used due to their ability to improve blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Panax ginseng are used due to their neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help prevent damage to brain cells.
remedy_recipe: Crush 500 grams of dried panax ginseng roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the ginseng powder with 750 grams of cellulose powder in a large bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules one by one for 10 minutes.
Label and date 1000 capsules containing 250 milligrams of the ginseng mixture each.
Store the herbal panax ginseng capsules in a cool dark place for up to 24 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a compound called tanshinone, which has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are believed to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Salvia miltiorrhiza capsules have been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, which is essential for maintaining healthy brain function. This improvement in blood flow can also help slow down cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Tanshinone: Tanshinone has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, contributing to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Salvianolic acid: Salvianolic acid has neuroprotective and anti-amyloidogenic properties, which may help prevent the formation of amyloid plaques and neuronal damage associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, thereby slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Salvia miltiorrhiza are rich in bioactive compounds like tanshinones, which are believed to have neuroprotective effects against Alzheimer's disease.

Leaves: The leaves of Salvia miltiorrhiza are used to prepare extracts that contain compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's.
Barks: The barks of Salvia miltiorrhiza contain tanshinones and other compounds that may help in improving blood flow to the brain and reducing symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia miltiorrhiza roots with a density of 2-3% of total root material in autumn.
Dry the roots in a single layer at 50-60°c for 2-3 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 10 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a grinder at 10,000 rpm.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 10 grams of vegetable cellulose in a capsule mold.
Fill 100 capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer at 10 kn.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are associated with the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, ginger has been shown to improve cognitive function and memory, possibly by increasing blood flow to the brain and promoting the clearance of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease. This may lead to improved symptoms and quality of life for individuals with the condition.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have been found to reduce beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, by inhibiting the production and accumulation of amyloid-beta peptides in the brain.

Gingerols and Shogaols: These constituents possess antioxidant properties, which help to protect neuronal cells from oxidative stress and inflammation, both of which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Volatile oils (especially 6-Gingerol): These compounds have been found to have neuroprotective effects, which may help to prevent or slow down the progression of neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's by reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of amyloid-beta peptides.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are rich in bioactive compounds that have potential neuroprotective effects, which can help in managing Alzheimer's symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have been studied for their potential therapeutic effects in Alzheimer's disease.

Barks: Although less commonly mentioned, some sources suggest that the barks of Zingiber officinale may also be used, possibly due to their content of bioactive compounds with neuroprotective properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the rhizomes in a warm place with good air circulation for 5-7 days.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder.
Mix 250g of the powder with 250g of vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill 500 empty capsules with the herbal powder mixture using a capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera capsules helps with alzheimer's disease because they contain the herb Ashwagandha, which has been shown to have neuroprotective properties. It helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are contributing factors to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Ashwagandha has been found to improve cognitive function and memory by enhancing the growth of new neurons and promoting the health of existing ones, potentially leading to improved symptoms and a better quality of life for those affected by the disease.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These compounds have been shown to possess neuroprotective properties, which can help prevent or slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

Withaferin A: This compound has been found to inhibit the production of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, by modulating the activity of certain enzymes involved in the disease's pathogenesis.

Saponins: These compounds have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the neuroinflammatory response and oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's disease, thereby slowing down its progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain bioactive compounds that help in neuroprotection and improve cognitive function.
Leaves: They have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Seeds: They contain bioactive saponins that may help in improving memory and cognitive function by modulating neurotransmitter activity and reducing neuroinflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest withania somnifera roots and leaves from a clean area to ensure purity and freshness.
Dry the harvested roots and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mill.
Mix the ground powder with a binding agent like gelatin and water in a ratio of 1:2.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them using a heat sealer.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound called carnosic acid. This compound has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, helping to slow down the progression of the disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. Additionally, the capsules may also improve cognitive function and memory by enhancing blood flow to the brain and promoting the growth of new neurons, ultimately helping to alleviate the symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Carnosic acid: It has neuroprotective properties, which help prevent neuronal damage and death, and may also help reduce beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.
Carnosol: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, and may also help improve cognitive function and memory.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, which have antioxidant and neuroprotective properties that may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Flowers: Used for their rosmarinic acid content, which has been shown to have potential benefits in reducing inflammation and improving cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.

Seeds: Not commonly used, but some sources suggest they may be used due to their rosmarinic acid content and potential neuroprotective effects, although more research is needed to confirm their efficacy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of rosmarinus officinalis leaves from healthy plants at peak potency during the morning.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 30°c for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of the ground powder with 250mg of a natural filler material like cornstarch.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herb-filler mixture and seal them immediately to maintain freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ocimum sanctum capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, a common hallmark of the disease. The capsules also contain compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function, enhance memory, and promote neuroplasticity, making it easier for individuals to learn and remember new information. This natural remedy has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to support brain health and may offer a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: This constituent has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Eugenol: Eugenol has neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help prevent neurodegeneration and reduce the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, and may also improve cognitive function and memory in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high content of essential oils, including carvacrol and thymol, which have neuroprotective properties.
Stems: Stems of Ocimum sanctum contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Roots: Roots of Ocimum sanctum are also used due to their richness in bioactive compounds like glycosides and alkaloids, which may have potential therapeutic effects on cognitive function and memory.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried leaves of the sacred herb from a trusted source under the morning sun.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mill with stainless steel blades for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 400g of gelatin in a bowl and allow the mixture to sit for 2 hours.
Fill a capsule machine with the gelatin-herb mixture and press 200 capsules with a consistent pressure.
Store the herbal capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months.
